title: Level 24
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 24: '''''"Extraterrestre"'''''}}{{Blurbg|file=Gaseous surface.jpg}}
{{Blurquote|quote-text=Where am I...?|image=Dreamscape 2.jpg}}{{Class 5e}}
==> Description == {{Blurimage|file=Space-art (level 24 rewrite).jpg|caption=An image of Level 24.}}
{{Blurimage|file=Space level 24 rewrite.webp|caption=Another said image of the level.}} '''Level 24''' is an infinitely desolate, liminal, dark, alien-like landscape. Rapid shifts and changes ensue chaos to the level. Furthermore, the level seemingly follows Euclidean geometry but completely ignores all known science, making '''Level 24''' unpredictable and dangerous. The level is seemingly a collection of alien and extraterrestrial landscapes enticed together, stretching on seemingly forever or at least too big to map out. Despite whatever variations occur in the atmosphere, landscape, gravity, and weather, the level retains its dark, gloomy appearance. This phenomenon is exceedingly intriguing, and currently unexplainable. The landscape becoming gaseous, temperature turning down to absolute zero, and gravity suddenly increasing or decreasing have been common hazards in the level. To elaborate further, another difference many wanderers reported is the variations in the sky. One may see clear constellations with towering planets that should not be scientifically possible at such close distance (May induce severe symptoms of Megalophobia to all wanderers); this phenomenon would usually mean said landscape lacks an atmosphere, but since '''Level 24''' completely ignores science, whether or not one can breathe in said environment depends on chance; other times atmosphere would thicken so much to the point of impeding vision, but one may suffocate on seemingly space void.
{{Blurquote|quote-text=Am I out of the liminal hell? Is this space...?|image=Dreamscape 2.jpg}}
{{Underline}} Occasionally, crystals of different colors can be found on and beneath the surface, each having unique properties depending on their coloration. These crystals are a source of intrigue for many wanderers, but also pose a significant danger due to their unpredictable nature. Similarities between the crystals found in [[Level 117]] and the crystals in said environment have been pointed out by many researchers, but no research have been done to provide conclusive evidence.
|-| Red = ===Description === The red crystal has a thermogenic effect, heating up surroundings efficiently and sometimes providing warmth. The amount of heat the crystal produces varies from one another. However, the crystal may cause burns if one is too close. |-| Blue = ===Description === The blue crystal has a cryogenic effect, cooling down its surroundings. Much like the red crystal, the decrease of temperature the crystal produces varies from one another. The crystal cannot harm wanderers unless touched, which may cause frostbite. |-| Green = ===Description === The Green Crystal, in contrast, has a consumable quality, and its unique flavor has been described as cucumber-lime. Once ingested, the crystal enhances and expedites the biological processes related to healing and immunity. Despite this, the Crystal is still very hard and requires good teeth in order to consume. |-| Yellow = ===Description === The Yellow Crystal is notable for its phosphorescent quality, continuously glowing without an external source of light. As a result, it is often utilized as a reliable light source in other backrooms levels. |-| Orange = ===Description === The Orange Crystal is particularly dangerous, capable of disintegrating organic materials upon contact and posing a severe hazard to wanderers. The M.E.G. has previously thought of weaponizing these crystals, but due to the dangers '''Level 24''' proposed, it is deemed too costly to gain. |-| Purple = ===Description === The Purple Crystal has a metaphysical effect, allowing a wanderer to experience the perspective of another wanderer within proximity who is also holding a purple crystal, creating an intimate and interconnected experience.
{{Collapse|num=6|initial='''> OPEN GALLERY'''|final='''> CLOSE GALLERY'''|body=
File:145g-natural-red-quartz-cluster-beautiful-red-aura-angel-crystal-cluster-reiki-Titanium-coating-cluster-healing-1183112801.jpg File:BU6Ff-4173859853.jpg File:85-95mm-Natural-translucent-green-fluorite-crystal-wand-dots-adorn-obelisk-feng-shui-chakra-healing-crystals-3720120058.jpg File:76a8a1912c04bd4cfa39263243e67316 large-2072667314.jpg File:BV03-a-1778628739.jpg File:Il fullxfull.1687161708 c5zc-1718238006.jpg
}}
{{Blurquote|quote-text=I walked on and on through the hopeless, dead, landscape...|image=Dreamscape 2.jpg}}
{{Underline}} Objects would fall from the sky at random intervals regardless of orbital range. Meteors and Comets are common hazards in '''Level 24'''. It is advised to take cover when such a phenomenon happens. Planets and other celestial objects in the sky can collide with each other on random intervals. This creates a similar effect, as debris will rain down into '''Level 24'''. ===Planetary Collision === {{Blurimage|file=Planetary Collision.jpg|caption=A artist's depiction of a Planetary Collision (may not be accurate).}} Astonishingly, planets seen on the horizon can also collide with the landscape. The collateral damage varies since '''Level 24''' completely disregards science (Creating huge impacts sending trillions of tons of rocks into orbit, touching the surface harmlessly like a beach ball on the water, or anything else are all possible scenarios). Although it is hard to envision such humongous objects crashing down, the landscape is so vast (possibly infinite) that such a phenomenon can happen. Information about the planetary collision is scarce because most wanderers who viewed it within a certain distance (even if far away enough to be unharmed) will go either blind or insane trying to comprehend such as massive structure. This phenomenon can cause intense fear to all wanderers regardless of symptoms of Megalophobia. === Void 001 === VO-001, also known as the lost planet, is a planet surrounded by a nebula and a object seemingly drifting in the void. Due to its identical properties to '''Level 24''', it is presumed that VO-001 is a small portion of '''Level 24''' that no-clipped into the heart of the void by unknown means. Further investigation has been conducted by several major groups, including [[Backroom Colonists|The Backroom Colonists]], The Kauer, and The Anti-Entity Group. Surprisingly, the most prominent faction, the [[M.E.G.|The M.E.G.]] has remained silent the matter. =='''>''' Entities == Though abnormal footprints have been found in the dust and rocks on ''''''Level 24'''''', suggesting there may be something "lurking about", no entities have been recorded present on ''''''Level 24''''''. Despite this, dreams of [[Phobia|Entity 12]] is almost certain if wanderers sleep on said level, which is highly unrecommended.
{{Blurquote|quote-text=Wait, I see something in the distance!|image=Dreamscape 2.jpg}}
==> Colonies and Outposts == {{Blurimage|file=Level 24 Black hole.webp|caption=A black hole in the horizon.}} Due to the environmental dangers and its difficulty to exit, setting up a base is incredibly foolish and near impossible. Despite this, [[M.E.G.|The M.E.G.]] has attempted a total of three mining expeditions to obtain the crystals in '''Level 24'''; however, the results were underwhelming and the project was abandoned. [[M.E.G.|The M.E.G.]], [[Backroom Colonists|The Backroom Colonists]], The Kauer, and The Anti-Entity Group have also sent exploration teams to the level, in the hopes of documenting '''Level 24''' to a greater extent, and temporary bases has been set up for this reason. The only team that returned, the Kauer's Omega squadron, discovered a structure anomaly seemingly inconsistent with the rest of the level, now designated as Situation 25.
{{Blurquote|quote-text=A glitching structure... it seems like I'm still stuck in the Backrooms...|image=Dreamscape 2.jpg}}
===Situation 25 === {{Blurimage|file=Level25.jpg|caption=The only image of this base.}} Situation 25 is an anomaly in '''Level 24''', discovered by The Kauer research organization. Situation 25 appears to be a futuristic structure reminiscent of the halls of Level 15. The exterior is seemingly transclucent while also glitching and shifting in appearance. Though Situation 25 may vary moderately in appearance, the structure retains its bright, clean, futuristic theme. While a small portion Situation 25 does appear above ground, most of the structure lies underground, sprawling expansively for unknown lengths. The interior surroundings seem to solidify as one enters; however, the interior is still highly unstable, and is prone to many shifts, glitches and other hazards. While still similar to level 15 in design, the interior structure seems slightly altered and modified to suit the nuturing of alien plant life. The artificial gardens within Situation 25 are a marvel to modern engineering. Despite being in a dimly lit environment, the LED lights that replace the sun provide enough energy to maintain the flourishing flowers and trees. Some sections with the overgrowth of canopy has created miniature forests of grassy paths, alien fauna, mutated redwoods, and even streams of clean drinking water. As one goes further in, despite still retaining the clean futuristic design, the condition of the area seems to deteriorate. Lights are dimer and flicker more frequently, the floor and walls may glitch more often, futuristic machinery starts to go haywire. The plant life becomes more decayed, as if mistreated or abandoned for a long time. The database suggests that wanderers not to approach the area, as the Kauer has only mapped out a small portion of the uppermost sections. Further expeditions into the deeper sections of Situation 25 have all failed to return.
{{Blurquote|quote-text=I'm getting tired... there's no food or water. I can feel my consciousness slipping away... |image=Dreamscape 2.jpg}}
==> Addendum ==
{{Collapse|num=1|initial=> Open B.C. file|final=> Close B.C. file|body=
''Several higher ups are seen discussing in a undisclosed level'' '''(Overseer of security) Darren Nakamura:''' There has not been this much casualties since the great war. All exploration squads sent to '''Level 24''' were compromised. Not to point fingers, but Laurel, you're responsible for this. '''(Overseer of exploration) Laurel Yew:''' This is hardly my fault, I TOLD you going into '''Level 24''' was a bad idea. We don't have the cohesion and professionalism of the M.E.G. or the Kauer! '''(Overseer of exploration) Laurel Yew:''' I don't think their sacrifice was in vain, we did receive signals that may imply information! '''(Overseer of regulation) Darren Nakamura:''' Yes... ''an SOS signal.'' ''Laughter'' '''(Overseer of exploration) Laurel Yew:''' (flustered) NO! You all don't get it! Don't you see? SOS signals are usually sent when under attack by a hostile presence! When was the last time someone sent a SOS signal for environmental dangers? According to data, SOS signals sent for hostile presence is up to 70 percent, while only 20 percent sent for environment dangers, 9 percent sent for miscellaneous reasons and 1 percent sent by accident. '''(Overseer of regulation) Lee Hollingsworth:''' What does this imply? '''(Overseer of exploration) Laurel Yew:''' That it is HIGHLY probable entities exist in '''Level 24'''! Especially when abnormal footprints have been found in the surface. Not to mention, we also received a voice message via grade 2 quantum messaging. '''(Overseer of development) Mitchel Texter:''' We did? '''(Minister) Chase X:''' (frowns) Why didn't you tell us? '''Lieutenant Morro:''' Sorry minister, it took a while for the message to be transferred, due to '''Level 24''''s distant and unstable nature. I received the message right before the meeting and reported this to Overseer Yew. '''(Overseer of security) Darren Nakamura:''' Work faster next time! '''(Overseer of exploration) Laurel Yew:''' (Rolls her eyes) You can't "work faster" to receive a message! '''Lieutenant Alan:''' At any rate, the message does bear pivotal information, the exploration leader reported spotting something abnormal in the distance. This message came from the same device that sent the SOS signal. '''Lieutenant Morro:''' Not to mention, this message was sent ''before'' the SOS signal, but due to its file size, we had a delay receiving said message. '''(Overseer of development) Mitchel Texter:''' What does this mean? '''(Overseer of exploration) Laurel Yew:''' If I were to guess, the group was attacked by some entity! Usually, one would not have a chance to even send a signal if environmental dangers got the better of them. '''(Minister) Chase X:''' Hard to say... I feel like we're missing some pieces of the puzzle here, Laurel, anything else to report? '''(Overseer of exploration) Laurel Yew:''' Uh, not as of now, no. '''(Minister) Chase X:''' Then I suggest we put this matter aside for now, we don't have enough evidence to offer something conclusive.
}} {{Collapse|num=3|initial=> Open Kauer file|final=> Close Kauer file|body=
''A blank room lights up, with a single operative sitting on a chair'' '''Artificial intelligence:''' Please state the purpose of your visit. '''Unnamed operative:''' I am here for the mission debrief of Situation 25. '''Artificial intelligence:''' Continue. '''Unnamed operative:''' Situation 25 at first bears a significant resemblance to Level 15, almost identical if not for some slight alterations. '''Artificial intelligence:''' That being? '''Unnamed operative:''' Mainly plants and more greenhouse technology, it is not ''exactly'' level 15, but too similar to be a sub level. '''Artificial intelligence:''' Continue. '''Unnamed operative:''' As my team went down deeper, the surroundings became darker, the condition of the base worsened. Plant life were decayed, as if left abandoned for a long time. There has even been broken down machinery lying around, some of the automatic gates were destroyed... saying the place was uncanny is an understatement. ''Silence, AI processing said information'' '''Artificial intelligence:''' Could Jovials be a possible cause of this condition? '''Unnamed operative:''' Perhaps, in fact, the surrounding were very similar to what a jovial infested area would be like, but it feels like the whole environment changed, not just the structure being destroyed, the deeper one went, the more paranoid one feels, as if we're being watched, yet heat signals didn't pick up anything near by.
}} {{Collapse|num=2|initial='''> Open A.E.G. file'''|final='''> Close A.E.G. file'''|body=
''Two shadowy figures meet up in a dark corridor'' '''AEG 1:''' The place where no sun shines. '''AEG 2:''' Level 6. ''The two figures shake hands'' '''AEG 2:''' This loss means more to us than the other factions. '''AEG 1:''' Indeed, we need to be more cautious next time. Some of our best men perished there. However, our path is clear. '''AEG 2:''' Our path is clear, all entities shall perish. '''AEG 1:''' We received nothing from the only exploration team we sent. However, one of the four groups is evidently hiding something. '''AEG 2:''' It couldn't be the Colonists could it? '''AEG 1:''' The group picking daisies and doing sing alongs in their treehouses? They would disband before hiding information from the database . . . no, I'm talking about the M.E.G. '''AEG 2:''' How can you be so certain? '''AEG 1:''' Don't you think they remained awfully quiet after the Kauer discovered Situation 25? Not to mention ignoring the lost planet. They're hiding something we don't know... '''AEG 2:''' Still, that is not enough reasoning to suspect them. '''AEG 1:''' According to inside sources, a M.E.G. squadron that survived was brought in for interrogation, yet everyone believes that the Kauer's Omega squadron was the only team that survived. Is that enough? '''AEG 2:''' What is it they're hiding that we don't know? '''AEG 1:''' I'm not sure, but especially with abnormal footprints found in the level, I suspect there is some enigmatic monster in need for elimination.
}} {{Collapse|num=4|initial='''> Open M.E.G. file'''|final='''> Close M.E.G. file'''|body=
''An Impresario walks inside a retro futuristic office room'' '''Impresario S:''' I’ve always wondered, we’re exactly are we? '''The First:''' (Chuckles) Does it matter? '''Impresario S:''' Well, curiosity always gets ahead of me, which level are we in? Level 154 wouldn’t make sense would it? '''The First:''' No, I suppose not... we’re not in a level, this is ah, my own domain. '''Impresario S:''' I’m not sure if I understand, but it doesn’t matter, I’m here to talk about ''this''. ''*The Impresario places down several files on the desk*'' '''Impresario S:''' I don’t get it, something is discovered in void, IN THE VOID! VO-001 obviously has ties with '''Level 24'''. Then there’s situation 25! Something is brewing! Three of the four major factions have been undertaking research, and we, the most prominent by far, just pretend we don’t know anything about it? '''The First:''' (amused) The Colonist have the most manpower, I’m sure they’ll take care of it. '''Impresario S:''' Sir, with all due respect, I’m not here to joke around, if anybody has the capacity to understand this, you know it's either us or the Kauer, the Colonists have always been abundant on resources, but lacks in organization and knowledge. What’s up with the act? You’ve avoided this subject during the council gatherings and we’re actively suppressing this information amongst our ranks for no reason at all! ''Silence'' '''The First:''' Sylas- '''Impresario S:''' Please, I’m one of your most trusted friends! ''The First slumps down on his chair, face twitches, slightly annoyed'' '''The First:''' It is futile to do any research related. '''Impresario S:''' Why? '''The First:''' Situation 25. '''Impresario S:''' What about it? '''The First:''' VO-001 is indeed a minuscule portion of level 25, it was cast into the void due to the migration of Situation 25… please, why do you think the base is so similar to level 15? ''Silence'' '''The First:''' (sighs) Because it is a part level 15. '''Impresario S:''' You mean it was a portion of level 15 just like VO-001 is to '''Level 24'''...? '''The First:''' No… to be exact, Situation 25 is still part of Level 15. A series of events somehow managed to no clip a small part of level 15 into '''Level 24''', if that makes sense… ''The Impresario abruptly leans forward, hands on the desk'' '''Impresario S:''' (surprised) That is possible? No-clippage of a level?! '''The First:''' Apparently yes, because of this, a portion of '''Level 24''' was replaced and consequently faded into nothingness, the void. This is the reason why the environmental dangers have not destroyed the base yet, because it's not really there. This portion of level 15 is in a rather complicated situation… you can imagine it being trapped in a limbo, or a purgatory, not much different from us being trapped in the backrooms. '''Impresario S:''' I- '''The First''': To put it simply, Situation 25 is still level 15, but just a part that only manifests its self in '''Level 24''', which consequently pushed a portion of Level 25 into the void, now designated as VO-001. '''Impresario S:''' Do the other Impresarios know of this? '''The First''': Some do, I have no qualms in informing all of them. However, I need some time to digest all this information myself… you see, the Kauer wasn’t the first to discover Situation 25. '''Impresario S:''' It was us, wasn’t it? '''The First''': (laughs) Surprisingly no, a team from the Colonists was the first, but they went in too far. Brave, but woefully unprepared… our squadron were the next to discover Situation 25. '''Impresario S:''' What made us more prepared? '''The First:''' The corporal was more cautious and contacted higher ups immediately, his squad stayed on the surface until further instructions. Eventually, a larger group made its way down into depths. ''Silence'' '''The First:''' Something was down there. '''Impresario S:''' Elaborate. '''The First:''' Hard to say, the members who came back were traumatized, none of them could recall the events in detail. Of course, all this information had to be suppressed. '''Impresario S:''' Why?! '''The First:''' The survivors seemed horrified about recounting the events. Some directly told the interrogators to "stay silent" on the matter. Researchers suggests that it is too much for a human brain to handle, and that discussing the event frequently might initiate some sort of cognitive hazard. Spreading the information may ah . . . cause further complications. '''Impresario S:''' What about the Kauer? '''The First:''' After said events, the Kauer discovered Situation 25 officially and safely documented it to the database. They didn't look deep enough, although further attempts to explore the area has all failed, I think they took the cue and canceled the project. The AEG and the Colonists are too far behind to complicate the situation. We are at a standstill right now, and it is in our best interest to keep it that way, it is the best that we leave that place alone. '''Impresario S:''' I- I see... '''The First:''' Now, let us have some tea shall we?
}}
==> Theories and Hypothesis == Theories on what the level truly is, origination, and other interpretations of this liminal environment.
The Titan Hypothesis
This theory suggests that the level is not infinite, but a huge anomalous planet estimated to be as big as a Dyson sphere. The surrounding planets, which are naturally smaller, all orbit and crash into the landscape. However, judging by the infinite maze like corridors of the backrooms, and its anomalous nature. It is highly improbable that '''Level 24''' is a planet instead of an anomalous liminal space.
The Infinity Thesis
The Infinity Thesis is the mainline explanation. This Theory suggests that the landscape is infinite, just like most spaces in the backrooms, and it, just like the rest, represents a unique liminal theme. This is further proven by the fact that the level always retains its dark desolate environment despite whatever changes occur. Since the landscape is infinite, meaning it is not a planet, some deem it as flat. However, this is not true, since no one can comprehend what shape infinity is.
The Outlander Approximation
This theory suggests that this level is one of the oldest levels in what we understand as the backrooms due to its primitive environment not resembling anything man-made. Although carvings on cave walls and relics have been found that are believed to be evidence of an ancient civilization.
The Many Worlds Construct
This theory suggests that '''Level 24''' is not one infinite landscape, but a collection of planets that wanderers no-clip to. This explains the extreme variations in environment, since wanderers are traversing differing planets. Smaller planets would constantly crash down to bigger ones while rotating each other in chaos, similarly to the three body problem, but to a far greater extent. However, there many counter arguments to this construct, such as the landscape wanderers enter in is always seemingly bigger than the planets in the sky, the extreme variations still occur in the same landscape that a wanderer no clips in, and the cohesive dark theme the environment always follows.
The Cognition Proposal
This theory suggests that everything in '''Level 24''' is up to the wanderer's interpretation, similarly to Bohr's proposal regarding quantum physics. '''Level 24''' could be all of the above, or none of the above. Everything is simply a reaction of the human psyche.
{{Blurquote|quote-text=That's it, I'm done for. No one is coming to help me.|image=Dreamscape 2.jpg}}
==> Entrances and Exits == ===Entrances=== * Main entrance: No-clipping from [[Level 23]]. * Finding the boarding room and noclipping through the door in [[Level 140]]. * Random no-clippage. ===Exits=== * Main exit: Although rare, wormholes may randomly appear, and entering any one of them will take you to [[Level 25]]. Do not enter black holes, however, as these will rip you apart. * No-clipping through glitching sections of the level. Theories suggest that one may no-clip to [[Level 15]] when going through the glitching walls of Situation 25. * Floating far enough into the vacuum space will take one to [[Level 700]] after a period of time. * [[Special:RandomInCategory/Levels|Random noclippage.]] File:Level.png|CORRUPTED IMAGE File:Space Nebula.jpg|A nebula in the sky File:Level 24 (gaseous).jpg|Surface becoming a giant gaseous storm File:Level 24 (gaseous 2).jpg|Another image of a gaseous surface
